blurb: PayPal deployed an Agentforce AI SDR agent against the roughly 8,000 monthly leads its 200-rep org was never going to work by hand, and meeting conversion is running about 50% higher than reps achieved alone. The agent runs a 10-nudge cadence on every stalled merchant, then hands a qualified, fully contexted lead to a human to close. Salesforce's advice for teams stuck on messy CRM data: don't fix everything first. Stand up a web agent on your site, FAQs, and product data while you consolidate the rest.

title: Ikea turns everyday objects into ingenious World Cup guessing game
blurb: Ikea Canada and Dentsu Creative launched "Assemble the World," a social-first campaign that rebuilds the flags of all 18 nations in the 2026 World Cup using everyday Ikea products and invites followers to guess the items. The play extends to OOH placements near stores through the summer, turning a trend-jacking moment into an interactive shopping prompt without abandoning the brand's flat-pack aesthetic.

title: Less Than a Quarter of Americans Use AI to Create or Edit Images
blurb: Fewer than one in four Americans (24%) use AI to create or edit images or videos, even as half of US adults now use AI chatbots and 44% specifically use ChatGPT, per Pew's February survey of 5,119 adults. The gap signals that AI image generation remains a niche behavior, so marketers leaning on AI visuals are still ahead of mainstream consumer adoption rather than chasing it.

title: Omnicom Chief Defends Interpublic Deal as AI Rattles Ad Industry
blurb: Omnicom CEO John Wren said the Interpublic acquisition, closed roughly seven months ago, is now delivering customer wins and cost savings even as AI pressures agency revenue. Wren still has to sell skeptical investors on the combined holding company's growth story as generative tools erode the production and media-buying work that agencies have long billed for.

title: Meta outlines best practices for AI-generated multimedia ads
blurb: Meta published best-practice guidance for its AI-powered ad creation tools, which generate multiple image, video, and text variations so advertisers can test combinations and surface the highest-performing creative. The pitch is volume-led optimization: let the system produce variants across formats, then let delivery data pick winners rather than hand-building each asset. For performance marketers, the practical move is feeding the tools strong source creative and clear brand constraints, since AI variation amplifies whatever input it starts from.

title: LinkedIn automates job application process for premium users
blurb: LinkedIn is rolling out a Premium Apply Assistant that uses AI to pre-fill job applications and auto-generate tailored cover letters for suitable roles, available to its premium subscribers. The shift lowers friction on applications and will likely spike volume per posting, pushing recruiters and employer-brand marketers toward sharper screening signals and differentiated job content to cut through the AI-generated noise.

title: Hightouch : pourquoi cette startup a fait une offre à 1,2 milliard de dollars à Publicis
blurb: Hightouch closed a $150M Series D from Goldman Sachs and Bain Capital Ventures at a $2.75B valuation, building a composable CDP that activates customer data sitting in Snowflake, BigQuery, and Databricks without the legacy baggage of Adobe or Salesforce. The company now claims $100M in ARR and over 1,000 customers including Spotify and Warner Music, and is pushing agentic AI that builds audiences and generates content variants across 300-plus channels.

title: Google releases new privacy controls for activity history, personalization
blurb: Google is splitting Web & App Activity into two new controls, Search Services History and Personalized Recommendations, giving users separate toggles for saved activity and personalized results across Search, Maps, Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Translate, and News. The change rolls out to Google Accounts over the next few days, but users with Web & App Activity already on will have the new media-saving option enabled by default.

title: Google adds AI guidance to Demand Gen campaigns
blurb: Google added AI-powered creative recommendations to Demand Gen campaigns, with Gemini suggesting how to optimize image and video assets for YouTube, alongside automatic video reformatting between vertical, square, and landscape and new insights into acquisition paths like app installs. Google also confirmed display campaigns start migrating into Demand Gen this month, with all new campaigns created through the Demand Gen flow later this year.

title: Instagram wants to monopolize your attention
blurb: Instagram launched a TV app for Fire TV, Google TV, and Samsung sets, adding disappearing Stories, horizontal YouTube-style video, and a coming push into longform episodic content and live creator experiences. The bet is that users will watch couch-bound what they normally scroll on their phones, a stationary format that breaks the always-in-pocket portability behind Instagram's 3 billion monthly users.

title: Meta plans to replace 90% of content review staff with AI
blurb: Meta plans to replace roughly 90% of its content review staff with AI, having already automated 50% of moderation with that share set to grow by year's end, per Axios. The shift lands despite a recent Instagram breach, and it signals to brand-safety teams that automated enforcement, not human reviewers, will increasingly decide what gets flagged, demoted, or pulled from feeds.

title: LinkedIn tests suggested feeds
blurb: LinkedIn is testing suggested feeds that surface posts from outside a user's network, echoing a similar alternative-feed experiment it ran last year. The change broadens exposure beyond first-degree connections, giving B2B marketers a potential path to organic reach without paying for it, while raising the risk that follower counts matter less as the algorithm decides who sees what.

title: New Writesonic data shows AI citations have 11–15 day shelf life.
blurb: A new Writesonic study tracking 23M cited sources across ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews found that 44% of cited pages vanish after a single appearance, with the typical citation lasting just 11 to 15 days. Perplexity holds sources roughly twice as long as ChatGPT, so GEO teams should treat citation durability, not one-time citation share, as the metric worth optimizing.

title: YouTube updates Shorts to make it even more like TikTok
blurb: YouTube updated Shorts with a TikTok-style "clear screen" mode, a heart icon replacing the thumbs-up, 2x hold-to-speed playback, and tap-to-mute, while dropping the public dislike button. The convergence narrows the creative and format gap between the two feeds, so brands can largely reuse vertical TikTok creative on Shorts and lean on "Not interested" signals rather than dislikes to read audience sentiment.

title: YouTube Shorts are getting even shorter with an update that lets you double the playback speed
blurb: YouTube is overhauling Shorts with a 2x playback speed setting, a "Clear Screen mode" that hides on-screen UI, a heart replacing the like button, and the removal of the dislike button in favor of "Not Interested" signals. The changes push Shorts toward faster consumption and softer engagement signals, narrowing the feedback marketers can read on creative and shifting optimization toward watch-completion on an asset already averaging 200B daily views.

title: YouTube Shorts nixes 'dislikes' and lets you double the playback speed
blurb: YouTube removed the dislike button from Shorts entirely, swapped the like for a heart, and added a TikTok-style Clear mode plus tap-and-hold double-speed playback across most regions. With Shorts now at 200 billion daily views, the loss of dislikes pushes creators and brands to read "Not interested" and "Don't recommend" signals instead, since the feed now tunes purely on those engagement controls.

title: LinkedIn automates job application process for premium users
blurb: LinkedIn launched Premium Apply Assistant, an AI feature that pre-fills job applications and drafts cover letters for roles it deems a good fit for premium subscribers. The move pushes LinkedIn deeper into agentic workflows on both sides of its marketplace, raising the prospect of AI-written applications meeting AI-driven recruiter screening with little human input between them.

title: Less Than a Quarter of Americans Use AI to Create or Edit Images
blurb: Pew's February survey of 5,119 U.S. adults found that fewer than a quarter use AI to create or edit images and videos, even as half now use chatbots at all and 38% of employed adults use them at work. The gap is a signal for PMs shipping generative-media features: text-based assistance has crossed into mainstream habit, while image and video generation remains a niche behavior most users have not yet adopted.

title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
blurb: Notion is shutting down Notion Mail across web, desktop, and iOS on September 22, ending the last remnant of its 2024 Skiff acquisition. The company says more than half of Notion Mail users already manage email without opening their inbox, handing the work to Notion agents, so it is redirecting the effort toward agent-run inboxes while leaving user email history in Gmail.

title: LLM-Based Discovery of Latent Requirements from Stakeholder Conversations: Preliminary Results from Industry
blurb: This paper introduces LENS, an LLM approach that reads stakeholder interview transcripts to surface not just explicitly stated requirements but the latent ones buried in how people describe their workflows and pain points. Across twelve industrial cybersecurity interviews, it hit an 84.4% F1 on explicit requirements, and domain experts judged 75% of the inferred latent requirements as offering real automation or time-saving value. For PMs running discovery, the signal is that the gap between what stakeholders say and what they actually need is becoming machine-tractable, with transcript excerpts linked back to each user story for traceability.

title: Notion Mail shuts down amid agent takeover
blurb: Notion is shutting down Notion Mail on September 22, retiring the standalone inbox in favor of agents that run email workflows directly. The company says more than half of Notion Mail users already manage email without opening their inbox, a signal that the product layer for inbox-heavy tools is shifting from UI toward agent orchestration.

title: TBM 427: The Bottleneck Strike Again!
blurb: The author argues that "engineering is no longer the bottleneck" is a flawed frame, because product development isn't a linear production line with one clogged pipe to clear. Drawing on Donella Meadows and Alicia Juarrero, the piece reframes constraints as distributed, interacting, and constantly co-evolving, where a visible bottleneck is usually a symptom rather than the problem. The practical takeaway for PMs: treat "the bottleneck moved" claims skeptically and look for the underlying system of constraints instead of chasing the latest logjam.

title: Rethinking Figma in an AI world
blurb: At Config 2026, Figma expanded its canvas into code layers, motion, shaders, and agent-driven workflows, a response to AI pulling design work into code editors and structured pipelines. The author argues the deeper threat is to Figma's seat model: if engineers can generate and inspect UI directly in a coding environment, the passive reviewer and inspector seats that anchored Figma's enterprise revenue lose their justification. The bet is that keeping the canvas at the center of product development survives an era where much of that work no longer needs a canvas at all.

title: How HappyFox Closed $1M in Expansion on a $20 AI Agent Spend with CEO Shalin Jain
blurb: HappyFox built an AI agent called Rex that reads every support ticket the moment it closes, flags expansion intent buried in the conversation, and routes it to sales. CEO Shalin Jain says the agent generated $1M in closed expansion revenue on under $20 in tokens, solving a structural gap where support reps see buying signals but never pass them on and AEs never read tickets. For PMs, the lesson is to mine existing customer data for intent rather than treating expansion as a reactive, hand-raise-only motion.

title: Mark Zuckerberg Directed Meta to Create a Prediction Markets App
blurb: Meta is building a standalone prediction markets app at Mark Zuckerberg's direction, pushing the company into a category where Kalshi and Polymarket have drawn regulatory scrutiny and fast user growth. The move signals Meta sees event-contract betting as a new engagement surface, and product teams should expect the usual platform pattern of a deep-pocketed incumbent entering a market that startups validated first.

title: YouTube Shorts nixes 'dislikes' and lets you double the playback speed
blurb: YouTube removed the dislike button from Shorts, swapped the like for a heart, and added a TikTok-style Clear Screen mode plus tap-and-hold double-speed playback, its most-requested feature. The dislike removal pushes ranking signal toward "Not interested" and "Don't recommend," a deliberate shift away from blunt negative feedback toward per-user controls that more precisely tune the recommendation feed.

title: Binance tells EU customers that it will stop providing services for them from July 1, after Greece rejected its application for a bloc-wide license last week (Financial Times)
blurb: Binance told EU customers it will halt services for them starting July 1, after Greece rejected its application for a bloc-wide MiCA license last week. The decision cuts off the exchange's regulated path into the EU market, forcing affected users to migrate to local entities or competitors as Binance regroups its licensing strategy across member states.

title: Binance tells EU users it will no longer provide services after failing to secure MiCA license
blurb: Binance told users across France, Italy, Poland, and Spain it will suspend services after failing to secure a MiCA license before the July 1 deadline, having already halted new EU registrations. The world's largest exchange withdrew its Greek license application and now plans to seek authorization in France, while assuring customers their assets remain accessible during the wind-down.

title: Senate races to advance crypto legislation in July as housing bill turmoil threatens timeline
blurb: The Senate is racing to advance the Clarity Act, the crypto market structure bill dividing oversight between the SEC and CFTC, with negotiator Cynthia Lummis expecting a floor vote in July after text circulates over the July 4 recess. A packed agenda of defense, farm, and contested housing bills before the August recess threatens to crowd out the limited floor time the legislation needs.

title: Japanese financial services giant SBI Holdings to buy Bitbank for $289 million
blurb: SBI Holdings agreed to buy Japanese crypto exchange Bitbank for around $289 million, with the deal subject to regulatory approval and set to close in October. The Tokyo-based financial group framed the purchase as a bid to expand its crypto business ahead of Japan's plan to bring digital assets under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act as early as next fiscal year.

title: The Web4 Agent Economy: A Large-Scale Empirical Study of the Landscape, Challenges, and Opportunities
blurb: A large-scale empirical study of the emerging Web4 agent economy analyzed 99,448 multi-chain identity registrations and over 317M transaction logs across protocols like MCP, x402, and EIP-8004. The authors found autonomous agents already process millions of daily machine-to-machine payments on immature infrastructure, with payment interoperability the most persistent unresolved bottleneck.

title: Invesco Files for Tokenized Stablecoin-Reserve Money Market Fund Built on Superstate Rails
blurb: Invesco, with $2.45T under management, filed with the SEC for the Invesco Stablecoin Reserves Onchain Fund, a Rule 2a-7 government money market fund whose shares are tokenized on a public blockchain and built to hold GENIUS Act-compliant stablecoin reserves. The fund relies on Robert Leshner's Superstate for third-party tokenization and transfer-agent rails rather than an in-house platform, joining JPMorgan and BlackRock in the 2026 race for tokenized reserve vehicles.

title: Spark, Uniswap, and Sky Launch $150M Liquidity Migration to Build Shared Stablecoin FX Layer
blurb: Spark, Uniswap, and Sky launched a shared Stablecoin FX Layer, beginning with a roughly $150M migration of USDS liquidity into Uniswap v4 USDS/USDT and USDS/PYUSD pools via a DualPool hook. Spark governs the allocation framework, redeploying idle pool liquidity across approved Sky products so capital earns yield while staying available for settlement across a multi-issuer stablecoin economy.

title: Coinbase's Base blockchain resumes after two-hour outage disrupted network
blurb: Base, the Coinbase-backed Ethereum layer-2, resumed block production Thursday after a roughly two-hour outage that halted transaction processing and block production across the network. The team attributed the disruption to an invalid block, advised node operators to restart their nodes, and said it is still investigating the root cause following a prior outage in August 2025.

title: $3 Million Reportedly Stolen in Polymarket Hack
blurb: A malicious script injected through a compromised third-party vendor drained roughly $3 million in pUSD, Polymarket's USDC-backed trading currency, from at least 11 users via a frontend phishing campaign, according to PeckShield. The attacker bridged the funds from Polygon to Ethereum and swapped them into about 1,893 ETH, and Polymarket says it has removed the affected dependency and will fully refund impacted users.

title: Tilikum: Transaction Fair Ordering on a DAG without Weak Edges
blurb: Tilikum is a DAG-based ledger protocol that enforces fair transaction ordering without weak edges, using median-based timestamp aggregation to block the reordering attacks that let adversaries extract BEV. DAG consensus protocols have scaled throughput but stayed largely unprotected against this class of attack. The Rust implementation reports up to 39x higher throughput than fair-ordering baselines like Themis and FairDAG while fully blocking known DAG-specific reordering attacks.

title: Aave founder Stani Kulechov says AAVE isn’t for sale ‘at a 70% discount’ following report of Payward bid
blurb: Aave founder Stani Kulechov denied a CoinDesk report that Kraken parent Payward is in talks to buy a 15% stake in the protocol at a $385 million valuation, calling it a "70% discount" to AAVE's fully diluted value. He did not rule out that Aave Labs could sell some of its token allocation through partnerships, while noting the protocol generates $134 million in annualized revenue now directed to the Aave DAO.

title: Trillion-dollar asset manager Invesco looks to plant a flag in tokenized stablecoin reserve sector
blurb: Invesco filed with the SEC to launch the Invesco Stablecoin Reserves Onchain Fund, a Rule 2a-7 government money market vehicle that lets GENIUS Act stablecoin issuers hold yield-bearing reserves in tokenized shares, with Superstate as sub-transfer agent. The $2.45T asset manager joins State Street, BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, BNY, JPMorgan, and Goldman Sachs in racing to capture the stablecoin reserve market.

title: Sharplink buys Ethereum for first time in 8 months, adding 5,000 ETH: onchain analyst
blurb: Sharplink bought 5,000 ETH worth $7.85 million on Thursday, its first ether purchase since October 2025, according to onchain data cited by EmberCN. The Ethereum treasury firm now holds 876,285 ETH worth roughly $1.3 billion at an average cost of $3,609, leaving it with an estimated $1.79 billion unrealized loss as ETH trades near $1,534.

title: Base delays Beryl hard fork over B20 registry timing issue
blurb: Base delayed its Beryl mainnet upgrade by one day to June 26 at 18:00 UTC, ensuring the B20 Activation Registry is fully operational before activation since the registry can take up to an hour to come online after the hard fork. Beryl introduces the native B20 token standard for stablecoin and RWA issuers, cuts the single-proof withdrawal delay to Ethereum from 7 days to 5, and integrates Reth V2 to reduce node storage overhead by up to 50%.

title: BitGo cuts 15% of staff to refocus on AI infrastructure and stablecoins
blurb: BitGo cut 15% of its staff to concentrate on security, trading, stablecoins, settlement, and AI-powered infrastructure, with CEO Mike Belshe saying no further reductions are anticipated. The cut follows a Q1 net loss that widened to $60.7M despite revenue rising 112.6% year-on-year to $3.8B, and mirrors AI-driven layoffs at Coinbase, Dune, and Block.

title: Prediction markets just posted their third straight record-setting week
blurb: Weekly prediction market volume hit a record $14.4B last week, up from $5-6B in January and more than 10x the level a year ago, while open interest set its third straight all-time high at $1.6B. Non-sports activity drove much of the gain, with politics, economics, and geopolitics markets on Kalshi and Polymarket reaching $3.6B combined, roughly 18x their July 2025 volume.

title: Too big to fail: Strategy’s $13 billion bitcoin paper loss alone dwarfs hundreds of prominent tokens
blurb: Strategy (MSTR) is sitting on more than $13B in unrealized bitcoin losses, having accumulated roughly 844,000 BTC at an average price near $75,600 against a spot price near $60,000, with the mark-to-market hit flowing straight through its income statement under fair-value accounting. The paper loss now exceeds the market caps of dogecoin, Cardano, Monero, and Chainlink, concentrating outsized single-asset risk in one public company's treasury.

title: zQR: A Verifiable QR-Driven zkSNARK Proof Verification Framework for Mobile Platforms
blurb: This paper introduces zQR, a framework that encodes zkSNARK proofs inside QR codes so offline verifiers can check them on mobile devices without a network connection. It pairs the QR transport with a blockchain layer for auditability and logging and uses LLMs to auto-generate the proving circuits. The evaluation reports proof generation and verification latency, QR encode/decode times, and on-chain gas costs, fitting proofs into a version-19 QR symbol at low error correction.

title: Story Rebrands as DATA Foundation in Pivot to AI Training Data
blurb: Story, the a16z-backed layer 1 built to put intellectual property onchain, rebranded as DATA Foundation and refocused on supplying provenance-tracked AI training data, migrating its $IP token to $DATA on a one-to-one basis. DATA jumped about 12% over 24 hours as the pivot retreated from Story's original IP-licensing thesis, whose total value locked has fallen to roughly $349,000 from a September peak near $45 million.

title: Polymarket says hackers stole users’ funds
blurb: Polymarket confirmed that hackers stole user funds after a third-party vendor breach let attackers inject malicious code into its website for some users, with blockchain firm PeckShield estimating roughly $3M drained from more than 11 victims via a phishing campaign. The prediction market said it has contained the incident and is refunding affected users in full.

title: Kalshi targets $40B valuation, nearly triple rival Polymarket’s $15B target, as prediction markets split in two
blurb: Kalshi is seeking fresh capital at a roughly $40B valuation, nearly double its $22B May 2026 Series F mark and almost triple the $15B target rival Polymarket is reportedly chasing. The gap reflects investor preference for Kalshi's CFTC-regulated model over Polymarket's blockchain-based exchange, as monthly trading volume on Kalshi recently passed $17B, up from about $5B a year earlier.

title: What Is the GENIUS Act? A Guide to U.S. Stablecoin Law
blurb: The GENIUS Act, signed into law on July 18, 2025, is the first comprehensive U.S. federal framework for dollar-backed payment stablecoins, setting rules on who can issue them, what reserves must back them, how holders redeem, and which regulators supervise issuers. The statute aims to keep dollar-denominated stablecoin activity onshore, countering the EU's MiCA regime and the patchwork of state money-transmitter licenses it replaces.

title: Multicoin predicts HYPE hits $319 by 2028 as Hyperliquid turns into an ‘everything exchange’
blurb: Multicoin Capital published a report arguing the market is severely mispricing HYPE at ~$63, projecting the token reaches ~$319 by 2028 on roughly $8B in annual earnings at a 20x multiple as Hyperliquid expands from a perp DEX into an "everything exchange." The firm, which has been aggressively buying HYPE since February, points to 2025 growth from 300,000 to 923,000 users, $873M in revenue, and $2.9T in trading volume.

title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
blurb: Polish authorities arrested four members of an organized cybercrime group accused of breaching telecom partners and running SIM-swapping attacks that drained crypto-exchange accounts of millions. Investigators estimate tens of millions of Polish złoty, at least $5M, was laundered through bank accounts and wallets across multiple countries, with blockchain investigator ZachXBT identifying one suspect as Wojtek Kulisz.

title: The Fungible Reserve Standard: A Deterministic Framework for Encoding Carrying Costs in Asset-Backed Tokens
blurb: This paper proposes the Fungible Reserve Standard (FRS), a token design framework that encodes the carrying costs of tokenized real-world assets, such as custody, insurance, and audit fees, directly into on-chain logic. Rather than rebasing, which breaks fungibility, FRS uses an asset-per-token variable q(t) that decays at a fixed annualized cost rate while a supply reconciliation mechanism preserves holder balances and ERC-20 composability. The design is asset-agnostic, applying to any RWA with predictable holding costs.

title: Strategy's yield-generating STRC stock is more correlated with BTC than ever
blurb: Strategy's STRC perpetual preferred stock has seen its 90-day correlation with bitcoin climb to nearly 0.70, the highest since the instrument's July 2025 debut, as STRC fell 23% this month to $76 against a roughly 20% bitcoin drop below $60,000. The tightening link erodes STRC's pitch as a steadier income vehicle, while its discount to the $100 par limits Strategy's ability to issue shares and buy more BTC.

title: Japanese financial services giant SBI Holdings to buy Bitbank for $289 million
blurb: SBI Holdings agreed to buy Japanese crypto exchange Bitbank for around $289 million, with the deal subject to regulatory approval and set to close in October. The Tokyo-based financial group framed the purchase as part of a push to expand its crypto business ahead of Japan reclassifying cryptocurrencies as financial products under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act, potentially from early next fiscal year.

title: Senate races to advance crypto legislation in July as housing bill turmoil threatens timeline
blurb: The Senate is racing to bring the Clarity Act, a crypto market-structure bill that would split oversight between the SEC and CFTC while giving the CFTC primary authority over most digital assets, to a floor vote in July, with Sen. Cynthia Lummis expecting a vote next month. A crowded pre-recess agenda including the defense and farm bills plus a contested housing package threatens to crowd out the legislation before August.

title: Binance tells EU users it will no longer provide services after failing to secure MiCA license
blurb: Binance told users across France, Italy, Poland, and Spain that it will restrict services after failing to secure a MiCA license before the July 1 deadline that requires authorization from at least one EU member state to operate across the bloc. The exchange withdrew its Greek application and now plans to seek a license in France, halting new EU registrations while it winds down unlicensed activity.

title: Binance tells EU customers that it will stop providing services for them from July 1, after Greece rejected its application for a bloc-wide license last week (Financial Times)
blurb: Binance told EU customers it will stop serving them on July 1, after Greece rejected its application for a MiCA license that would have passported across the bloc. The rejection blocks Binance from operating in the EU under the single-license regime, forcing the exchange to exit one of its largest markets while rivals that secured MiCA authorization move to capture displaced users.

title: Invesco Files for Tokenized Stablecoin-Reserve Money Market Fund Built on Superstate Rails
blurb: Invesco filed with the SEC to launch the Stablecoin Reserves Onchain Fund, a Rule 2a-7 government money market fund whose shares are recorded as tokens on a public blockchain and built on Superstate's transfer-agent rails rather than an in-house platform. The fund holds only GENIUS Act-eligible reserves with a 93-day maturity ceiling, joining JPMorgan and BlackRock in the race to back stablecoins with tokenized money market funds.

title: Coinbase's Base blockchain resumes after two-hour outage disrupted network
blurb: Coinbase-backed Base, one of Ethereum's largest layer-2 networks, resumed block production Thursday after a roughly two-hour outage in which an invalid block halted transaction processing. The team is still investigating the root cause and has advised node operators to restart their nodes, marking the network's second disruption since an August 2025 outage.

title: GCash Owner Said to Seek $1.5 Billion in Record Philippine IPO
blurb: Mynt, the parent company of Philippine mobile wallet GCash, is seeking to raise as much as 92.3 billion pesos ($1.5B) in what would be the country's largest IPO ever. The listing signals investor appetite for Southeast Asian fintech at scale, where mobile wallets have outpaced traditional banking infrastructure as the default rail for payments and remittances.

title: Trillion-dollar asset manager Invesco looks to plant a flag in tokenized stablecoin reserve sector
blurb: Invesco filed with the SEC to launch the Invesco Stablecoin Reserves Onchain Fund, a Rule 2a-7 government money market fund for stablecoin issuers to hold GENIUS-compliant reserves while earning yield, with Superstate tokenizing its shares. The $2.45T asset manager joins State Street, BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, BNY, JPMorgan, and Goldman Sachs in racing to capture issuer reserves now that the GENIUS Act defines eligible backing assets.

title: Memory maker SK hynix files for $29B US IPO amid AI demand
blurb: SK hynix filed to list on the Nasdaq for up to $29.4 billion, the second-largest US IPO on record behind SpaceX, on the strength of its dominant HBM memory business. The world's largest HBM supplier posted 198% year-over-year revenue growth to $38 billion in Q1 at a 77% net margin, making the listing a direct bet on sustained AI-chip demand.

title: Aave founder Stani Kulechov says AAVE isn’t for sale ‘at a 70% discount’ following report of Payward bid
blurb: Aave founder Stani Kulechov denied reports that Kraken parent Payward is negotiating to buy a 15% stake in the lending protocol at a $385M valuation, which he said would price AAVE at a 70% discount to its fully diluted value. He left open the possibility that Aave Labs could sell some of its token allocation through partnerships, even as the protocol generates roughly $134M in annualized revenue now routed to the DAO.

title: BitGo cuts 15% of staff to refocus on AI infrastructure and stablecoins
blurb: BitGo cut 15% of its workforce to concentrate on security, trading, stablecoins, settlement, and AI-powered infrastructure, CEO Mike Belshe said, adding that no further reductions are anticipated. The cuts follow a Q1 in which revenue rose 112.6% year-over-year to $3.8B but net losses widened to $60.7M, and they echo recent AI-driven pivots at Coinbase, Dune, and Block.

title: SoFi Launches SOFIUSD Stablecoin on Solana
blurb: SoFi launched SOFIUSD, a dollar-backed stablecoin issued on Solana, extending its national bank charter into onchain settlement. The move lets SoFi route payments and stored value across a public blockchain rather than relying solely on ACH and card rails, and positions a chartered bank to compete directly with issuers like Circle on cross-border and programmable-money use cases.

title: Too big to fail: Strategy’s $13 billion bitcoin paper loss alone dwarfs hundreds of prominent tokens
blurb: Strategy is carrying more than $13B in unrealized bitcoin losses, having acquired roughly 844,000 BTC at an average near $75,600 against a market now near $60,000, with fair-value accounting routing the mark-to-market hit straight through its income statement. The paper loss alone exceeds the market caps of dogecoin, Monero, Cardano, and Chainlink, underscoring the concentration risk of locking corporate capital into a single asset.

title: Musk Starts Expanding X Payments to More Users After Delay
blurb: Musk began expanding X Money to a subset of premium X users after repeated delays, an early step toward his "everything app" ambition that bundles payments into the social network. The rollout puts X in direct competition with established P2P and wallet players like PayPal and Cash App, though it still depends on state money-transmitter licensing and bank partnerships to scale settlement nationally.

title: AIRWALLEX lève 320 millions de dollars et ouvre un nouveau front face à Stripe
blurb: Airwallex raised $320M in a Series H led by Addition at an $11B valuation, up from $8B six months ago, with backers including Baillie Gifford, QED, and Amex Ventures returning. The Australian cross-border payments firm is repositioning from a multi-currency infrastructure specialist into an AI-native financial operating system, opening a new competitive front with Stripe as it reports $1.3B in annualized revenue, up 74% year over year.

title: Alan lève 480 millions d’euros et atteint 5,5 milliards d’euros de valorisation
blurb: Alan raised €480M at a €5.5B valuation, led by Prosus with Dara Holdings joining and existing backers Teachers' Venture Growth and Index Ventures participating, just three months after a €100M round. The French health-insurance scale-up reports €787M in ARR, up 54% year-over-year, and reached profitability in France in 2025 while still posting a €26M group loss as it funds acquisitions and international expansion.

title: UK Finance and banks collaborate on digital verification service
blurb: UK Finance is backing Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds, Nationwide, NatWest and Santander to build a bank-led digital verification service that lets customers confirm details like name, age or address through their banking app, with verified data shared to third parties only on explicit consent. The project has cleared proof-of-concept work using synthetic data and moves to a live pilot in the coming months, with Select ID leading technical design and delivery.

title: What Is the GENIUS Act? A Guide to U.S. Stablecoin Law
blurb: The GENIUS Act, signed into law in July 2025, is the first comprehensive U.S. federal framework for dollar-backed stablecoins, setting rules for who can issue payment stablecoins, what reserves must back them, how holders redeem, and which regulators supervise issuers. The statute pulls stablecoin oversight out of a state-by-state money-transmitter patchwork and into a supervised federal regime, a direct answer to the EU's MiCA framework that lawmakers feared would push issuance offshore.

title: Exclusive: British Business Bank backs 10 VC funds with £90m pot
blurb: The British Business Bank committed up to £90m to 10 first-time UK VC funds, its first deployment from the £500m package announced in July 2025 to back fund managers from underrepresented backgrounds. Each fund runs £10m to £20m and targets pre-seed and seed deeptech, defence, and climate startups, with the BBB anchoring roughly 50% of each and GPs that are 57% women and 43% from ethnic minority backgrounds.

title: Kalshi targets $40B valuation, nearly triple rival Polymarket’s $15B target, as prediction markets split in two
blurb: Kalshi is seeking fresh capital at roughly a $40B valuation, nearly triple the $15B that blockchain-based rival Polymarket is reportedly chasing, with the gap reflecting investor preference for the CFTC-regulated model. The federally regulated exchange recently saw monthly trading volume pass $17B, up from $5B a year earlier, and its CEO confirmed on June 24 it is weighing an IPO unlikely before 2027.

title: David Marcus on Lightspark reaching escape velocity in global payments
blurb: David Marcus said Lightspark has hit "escape velocity" in global payments four years in, with payment systems in nearly 70 countries integrated for real-time settlement, every major stablecoin and blockchain live, and a Visa stack integration for global card issuance. Marcus framed the milestone as the convergence of licensing, capabilities, and partnerships that most payments companies abandon before reaching, anchored by Lightspark's Spark stablecoin and Bitcoin settlement rail.

title: US Stocks Post First Outflow Since March as Tech Trade Falters
blurb: US equities recorded their first net outflow since March, with record withdrawals from tech funds as the AI trade that drove this year's rally cools. The reversal signals fading conviction in megacap tech and AI infrastructure names, and could tighten funding conditions for fintechs and growth startups that have leaned on buoyant public-market valuations to raise and exit.
